references.title: Difficulty aware programming knowledge tracing via large language models

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Predicting next programming-task success from historical submissions
extracted.Features: Problems, submission responses, CodeBERT embeddings, LLM-generated KCs, TD, KD
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Prior programming KT insufficiently models difficulty, especially TD vs KD
extracted.Novelty of Model: Difficulty-aware programming KT with GPT-4o + CodeBERT + GAT + update gate
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Max sequence length 50; split or zero-pad sequences; 5-fold CV; Adam, lr 0.001, batch 32, epochs 50
extracted.Models: DKT, DKVMN, SAKT, EKT, Code-DKT, DKT+, DIMKT, QIKT, TCKT, THKT, DPKT
extracted.Comments: Distinguishes text difficulty from knowledge difficulty, which is the main conceptual contribution
extracted.Limitations: Needs externally generated difficulty labels; long-term state modeling and richer behavioral features remain future work

annotations.text:
KT techniques, while good at achieving good accuracies on different benchmark datasets, have often not focused on the impact of difficulty in knowledge modeling. Authors propose a Difficulty-Aware Programming Knowledge Tracing via Large Language Models (DPKT) that aims to improve at this component that is so important for open-ended questions such as programming challenges. 

Results show that Model performs excellently across different Language Datasets.
